current: Choose cross-fitted witness estimators hat_g_1n, hat_g_0n, and hat_e_n attaining the rates in ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e. Then for every law P satisfying ass:consistency, ass:ignorability, ass:right-overlap, ass:resid-treated, ass:resid-control, ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e, and in particular for every P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) or P in W_n^reg(r_0n, r_1n, r_en), and every deterministic lambda in Lambda_n, E_P[psi_lambda(O; g_0, g_1, e)] = tau and |hat_tau_lambda - tau| = O_P(U_n(lambda)).
proposed: Choose cross-fitted witness estimators hat_g_1n, hat_g_0n, and hat_e_n attaining the rates in ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e. Then for every law P satisfying ass:consistency, ass:ignorability, ass:right-overlap, ass:resid-treated, ass:resid-control, ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e, and in particular for every P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) or P in W_n^reg(r_0n, r_1n, r_en), and every deterministic lambda in Lambda_n, E_P[psi_lambda(O; g_0, g_1, e)] = tau and |hat_tau_lambda - tau| = O_P( n^(-1/2) [ E[e_lambda(X)^(-1)]^(1/2) + lambda^(-1) r_1n + r_0n + lambda^(-3/2) r_en + r_en ] + pi(lambda)^(1/2) r_1n + lambda^(-1) r_1n r_en + r_0n r_en ).
reason: The exact unbiasedness statement is provable, but the displayed envelope U_n(lambda) is too small under the frozen assumptions. Once the feasible score uses the estimated clipped denominator hat_e_lambda inside inverse weights, the evaluation-sample fluctuation contains extra terms that are not controlled by U_n(lambda) from only L2(P_X) nuisance rates. In the proof decomposition, the treated plug-in term contributes a centered fluctuation of order n^(-1/2) lambda^(-1) ||hat_g_1n-g_1||_{L2(P_X)}, and the treated denominator-error residual contributes a centered fluctuation of order n^(-1/2) lambda^(-3/2) ||hat_e_n-e||_{L2(P_X)}. These terms do not collapse to pi(lambda)^(1/2) r_1n + lambda^(-1) r_1n r_en without additional weighted-norm, relative-error, or high-probability stabilization assumptions that are absent from the frozen core. The proposed statement is the honest upper bound obtainable from the declared dependencies.

current: Let lambda_dagger_n be any deterministic selection satisfying def:near-optimal-threshold. Then the lower-bound handle H_{n,lambda_dagger_n} can be completed on the subclass W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) into a four-point Le Cam / Assouad family with pairwise chi-square distances uniformly bounded by a universal constant and with inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^* for some universal c > 0.
proposed: Let lambda_dagger_n be any deterministic selection satisfying def:near-optimal-threshold. Suppose the handle H_{n,lambda_dagger_n} is completed into a four-point family {P_{s,t} : (s,t) in {-1,1}^2} contained in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) such that the pairwise chi-square distances between the n-sample product laws P_{s,t}^{\otimes n} are bounded by a sufficiently small universal constant C_chi < 4 and the parameter diameter satisfies max_{(s,t),(s',t')} |tau(P_{s,t}) - tau(P_{s',t'})| >= c_0 R_n^* for some universal c_0 > 0. Then inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^* for some universal c > 0.
reason: The frozen core does not prove the constructive part of the current claim. The assumptions ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e are upper-side estimability statements, not a common hard nuisance class or a least-favorable local submodel, so they do not imply that the perturbations in H_{n,lambda} remain inside W_n^lb or that they realize the scales r_1n, r_0n, r_en as lower-bound separations. More concretely, with the amplitudes fixed in the handle, the regular-cell mean shift has size delta_0n(lambda) ~ r_0n because P_X(B_lambda) is bounded below, so under a natural Gaussian or bounded-variance completion its n-sample chi-square contribution is of order n r_0n^2, which need not stay bounded for admissible sequences. Likewise the shell logit perturbation has L2-normalized size u_1n(lambda) ~ r_en / lambda^{1 + kappa/2}, giving a propensity chi-square scale of order n r_en^2 / lambda, again uncontrolled by the frozen assumptions. The frozen core also does not derive the needed tau-diameter of order R_n^* from the handle. What is provable from the frozen core is the information-theoretic reduction: once a completed family with small product chi-square and diameter c_0 R_n^* is supplied, a standard Le Cam argument yields the minimax lower bound.

current: The minimax absolute risk for tau over W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is equivalent, up to universal constants, to R_n^* = inf_{lambda in Lambda_n} R_n(lambda).
proposed: There exists a universal constant c > 0 such that inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^*. Moreover, if the upper theorem is strengthened to a uniform absolute-risk bound, namely if there exist a universal constant C > 0 and a deterministic sequence lambda_n in Lambda_n with R_n(lambda_n) <= 2 R_n^* such that sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau_{lambda_n} - tau(P)| <= C U_n(lambda_n), then the minimax absolute risk over W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is equivalent, up to universal constants, to R_n^*.
reason: The lower-bound direction is provable because conj:tail-lower-bound applies on the subclass W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en), and that subclass is contained in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en). The upper direction is not derivable from the declared dependencies as stated. thm:clipped-upper gives only the pointwise stochastic-order conclusion |hat_tau_lambda - tau| = O_P(U_n(lambda)); it does not give a uniform-over-P bound on E_P |hat_tau_lambda - tau|, which is the quantity entering minimax absolute risk. prop:tail-order-reduction compares U_n(lambda) and R_n(lambda), but it does not upgrade pointwise O_P control to a uniform L1 risk bound. So the unconditional equivalence claim is stronger than what the frozen upper theorem currently supplies.

current: Over E_n(a_0, a_1, a_e, kappa), the conjectured minimax absolute rate is n^(-nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, kappa)) when kappa != 1. When kappa = 1, the conjectured rate is n^(-nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1)) if nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1) < 1/2, and n^(-1/2) (log n)^(1/2) if nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1) = 1/2. One-sided overlap decay strictly changes the bounded-overlap structure-agnostic benchmark min{1/2, a_1 + a_e, a_0 + a_e} exactly when kappa < kappa^*(a_0, a_1, a_e), while kappa = 1 is a logarithmic boundary only in the root-n regime nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1) = 1/2.
proposed: Over E_n(a_0, a_1, a_e, kappa), the conjectured minimax absolute rate is n^(-nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, kappa)) when kappa != 1. When kappa = 1, the conjectured rate is n^(-nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1)) if nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1) < 1/2. If nu(a_0, a_1, a_e, 1) = 1/2, then the rate is n^(-1/2) when the fixed-threshold choice t = 0 already attains the supremum in def:phase-exponent (equivalently, a_1 >= 1/2), and it is n^(-1/2) (log n)^(1/2) when every maximizing t is strictly positive. One-sided overlap decay strictly changes the bounded-overlap structure-agnostic benchmark min{1/2, a_1 + a_e, a_0 + a_e} exactly when kappa < kappa^*(a_0, a_1, a_e).
reason: The unconditional kappa = 1 root-n logarithm is false under the frozen definitions. A counterexample is a_0 = a_1 = a_e = 1: then nu(a_0,a_1,a_e,1) = 1/2, but taking the fixed grid point lambda = lambda_0 gives R_n(lambda_0) = n^(-1/2) + O(n^(-1)), so the frontier is order n^(-1/2), not n^(-1/2)(log n)^(1/2). The log factor appears only when achieving exponent 1/2 requires a strictly shrinking threshold, not when the fixed threshold already attains the benchmark.
